reviews.review_text: Such an awesome park very clean the ensuite sites have the fake grass that makes it easier to stay clean. Beach is next door and a short walk up to cafe,bakery or IGA. Team were very accommodating to our request nothing was a hassle. Will be back

We had a wonderful stay in a generous cabin overlooking the river. An easy five minute walk to the beach, and shops close by, too. The suburb has a lovely feel to it. Lots of friendly people as we went to the pub, club and various shops. Really most things were easy walking distance. A wonderful place for families of any stage in life. Lots of parks at site and around within a short walk. The Native Botanic Gardens were beautiful. Ten minute walk and a park for kids included.

Our bikes were stolen from this caravan park in April 2026 after someone cut through the chain and took them. I’ve since learned that many others have experienced bike theft here as well. The level of theft is alarming, and it creates a dangerous, unsettling environment - especially for families with children staying at the park.

It's such a shame because we have stayed here a few years ago and really enjoyed the stay.
Great location you can listen to waves crashing as you sleep. Clean amenities and always a nice cool breeze
Great place to relax

We stayed over the weekend. The design of the power site is very easy to reverse in my 21ft caravan. Clean pool and amenities. Friendly staffs at reception. Didn't have time to enjoy much. Will visit again next time. Very friendly people in the permanent cabin

If I could give more than five stars I would! We camped at an ensuite site. The ensuite was immaculate! Well appointed and beautifully clean. The park is exceptionally well maintained and staff very friendly and always happy to help
Location is unsurpassed in my view
Always our destination for rest and recovery from life!

We stayed here two weeks ago and loved it we extended our stay for an extra few nights even though we had to move sites as it was a busy weekend. The park is lovely , amenities were kept clean, pool was beautiful and clean , easy walk to the town and beach which is beautiful.  Reception staff were helpful and friendly.  We would definitely return here.

Park your car every thing is within a 5 mins walk.
Look out for the international airport. Oh its for birds from all over the world

We spent one night here in our camper van. The price is quite high for one night, but we had a powered site, the parking space is very large, right by the sea, very clean sanitary facilities, and friendly staff. Otherwise, the site is definitely well suited for families with children.
I would go here again !

A great place to escape the bussle of life. Easy access to the beach and the river. The facilities are very good and the unsuited caravan sites are well kept and clean.
Enjoy a walk on the beach, watch the sun set over the river, relax to the sound of the waves at night and go and explore Berry, Kangaroo Valley and the Nowra and Jervis Bay region.

Very clean amenities. Great size pool. Bonus toddler pool. Staff were exceptionally helpful when we became stranded without a car and had to extend our stay. Park was clean. Great holiday activities provided for the kids. Will definitely come back.
This caravan park is pretty kool but the weather is four seasons in one day

​I cannot recommend this park based on our recent Christmas stay. While the booking process was pleasant, the actual service on-site was unprofessional and unsupportive.
​The sites are quite cramped compared to other parks, requiring us to park our car outside the gates. Furthermore, the environment felt poorly managed; we witnessed young children vaping in the public toilets near the pool area.
​Most concerning was the theft of two valuable items from our site. When we approached management for help, they were dismissive and refused to check their security cameras to assist us. The lack of accountability and poor customer service truly ruined our holiday. We will not be returning.

​I cannot recommend this park based on our recent Christmas stay. While the booking process was pleasant, the actual service on-site was unprofessional and unsupportive.|​The sites are quite cramped compared to other parks, requiring us to park our car outside the gates. Furthermore, the environment felt poorly managed; we witnessed young children vaping in the public toilets near the pool area.|​Most concerning was the theft of two valuable items from our site. When we approached management for help, they were dismissive and refused to check their security cameras to assist us. The lack of accountability and poor customer service truly ruined our holiday. We will not be returning.

Staff very helpful.|clean, neat tidy park close to beach & inlet so great location. Walking distance to pub which has great meals at reasonable prices. Easy access when booking, with email sent with details of gate code (also used for amenities) & site number. Check in is from 11am for caravan site. Lots of onsite vans but doesn’t feel crowded. A great park to stay at.

The staff is nice and helpful. We're given a late check in with proper instruction and also a late check out on a weekday with no extra fees. The Meroo cabin size is okay, but kitchen isn't all clean (dirty utensils). They provided small travel size bottles of shampoo and body wash but that's all. If staying longer than 2 days with more than 2 people, better prepare your own. Toilet sink is really small but I guess it's useable.

Easy walk to the beach :)

Had such a wonderful time at Holiday Haven Shoalhaven Heads! The park is super clean, peaceful, and in the best location right near the beach and river. Highly recommend it for anyone wanting a relaxing getaway!
Brilliant as always. Ensuite bathrooms on sites are very good quality, staff try hard to help in any way they can.

Hi I’m Aura and I’m 11
And I loved my experience here down at shoalhaven .

They had lovely cabins with 2 bedrooms and a nice bathroom .
Since we had a lot of little kids around, there were heaps of stuff to entertain them!

The pool was nice and also provides a kiddie play splash park .

The playground was good and every kid had fun , had a jumping pillow and every kid loved when it was deflating

Tip for the playground:
I run around there barefoot and the place if filled with bark and it hurts my feet , so please find something else that is less spiky

Overall I LOVED it thank you for having a great place

Great location, excellent ensuite site for our caravan.. also dog friendly.
Staff are very welcoming and helpful.
Even got to see some of the local bandicoot population at night hopping around near the vans.😎
Locals at the various businesses (grocers, bottle shop, takeaway etc.) were also friendly and pleasant to deal with.
We will definitely be back.

What a beautiful park this is. We were lucky enough to be able to spend a few days with our grandson at this wonderful park. What a lovely stay. Location is great. Park is clean and tidy. Pool, jumping pillow, park was so much fun. He enjoyed the bike tracks . The staff a super nice. What a beautiful part of the world this is. Thank you

Nice  location  on  Shoalhaven  inlet  and  adjacent  beach, caravan  sites  good  sized, good  grassed  throughout  park, amenities  modern  ladies, old  but  clean  men's, heaps  of  kids  play  areas, beautiful  large  pool, tennis  court, mini  golf, jym area. Small  shops  nearby and  about  15 minutes  drive  into  Nowra

We had an absolutely wonderful stay at Holiday Haven Shoalhaven Heads! The location is perfect—nestled between the river and the beach, it offers beautiful views and a peaceful atmosphere. The park is incredibly clean and well-maintained, with spacious cabins and great facilities. The staff were warm, welcoming, and always ready to help, which made the experience even more enjoyable.

There are plenty of activities for all ages, from the swimming pool and playground for kids to scenic walks and fishing spots nearby for adults. It’s a fantastic place for families, couples, or anyone looking to relax and unwind in a natural setting. We’ll definitely be back and highly recommend it to anyone visiting the Shoalhaven area!

I want to start by saying that the place was great. Located walking distance to the beach, our daughter loved the play equipment and the room was clean, looked after, and well equipped for what we needed. It was easy to find, good food places around with the Bowling Club and Pub close by and an easy drive into Nowra to visit the Wildlife Park.

However we were disappointed with the response we received the week before.
We called a week earlier as our daughter was in hospital and we didn't know what was happening and when we would get out. We asked for a credit as we still wanted to come down but it was all unknown. The reply was no, we are within 14 days and that's our policy. A cold response. I understand businesses need cancellation policies, and during a peak period I would have copped the loss if we couldn't attend, however it was a mid week visit, in a school term, the weather was cooling and it was pretty much empty when we visited.
We had to end up leaving a day early to return to work due to the previous week.

The business may have held onto a sale by enforcing the cancellation policy, but they have potentially lost a returning customer as we will look elsewhere for our short getaways. As a business it's a fine line you need to juggle and I felt this one missed the mark.

Nice park, and enjoyed being  along fence across from river… neat and tidy, well maintained. A large park mainly made up of permanent caravans. Clearly cabins and permanents are the main focus. Nice facilities like pool, mini golf etc, HOWEVER disappointing that there are no bins throughout park- just two points large bins.; and dump point way up one end albeit in a parking bay suitable for washing cars and boats.
Great location, most clean caravan park i have ever been to. Very tidy and great amenities. Highly recommend.

My family stayed 2 nights on camp-site 343 , location was great extremely close to beach access . The kids loved the jumpy pillow, pool and riding the bikes around playing with their cousins and friends. The only negative would be my 2 year old was happily riding her balance bike when a dog chased her barking at her and scared her terribly . The owner of the dog did come and apologise but it was to much of a close call for my liking . I think it's fine to have dogs there but aslong as they are always secure and friendly .
Next time we will stay for longer as it was great for the family .

Great caravan park for families.
Jumping pillow, swimming pool, climbing equipment, pirate ship, clean amenities,  camp kitchens throughout the park.
Cabins both riverside and dune cabins available, and campsites powered and ensuite sites too.

Local shops situated before the park entry.
Beach on one side,  and river on the other.
Boat ramps also within minutes of the park.
Awesome park will be back

We stayed here for just under 2 weeks. It gets super busy during peak periods. Great jumpy pillow, amazing pool, great facilities and an awesome games room. We were parked up alongside the river but it was also an easy walk to the ocean and the shops. Great stay and I highly recommend

Site 367 don't! Large campground but crammed in like sardines, whatever you do don't stay 364-367 crammed in against the fence and have to drive several rows back to get there, when busy a nightmare. Sadly we had site 367 tiny couldn't park a Ford Ranger front to curb without overhang to the path due to branches overhanging the fence, we paid 3 nights but stayed 1 😒

Site 367 don't! Large campground but crammed in like sardines, whatever you do don't stay 364-367 crammed in against the fence and have to drive several rows back to get there, when busy a nightmare. Sadly we had site 367 tiny couldn't park a Ford Ranger front to curb without overhang to the path due to branches overhanging the fence, we paid 3 nights but stayed 1 😒

Beautifully present park. Swimming pool was always clean, as was the playground and surrounding park areas.
Lots of on-site vans that didn’t appear to be utilised, perhaps as the end of the school holidays is close.
The park borders the ocean and inlet, very pretty area.
Beach to one side, river the other! Great pool, playground, and games room.

They placed  us in a spot where camper vans and trailers were supposed to be. our tent got Ripped because of the wind and there was no trees around any spots when it was boiling hot. We asked for a refound because we left 2 days before and still no refound for all of this. Drfinetly will never be coming here ever again, and do not recomand comiming to this campsite.
We can definitely recommend staying at Holiday Haven Shoalhaven Heads.  Spotless amenities and great pool.  The park is close walking distance to the beach and river.  Perfect location 👌.
The relaxing atmosphere is great for the kids, and the pool, tennis court, and play area has a big bouncing area walking distance to bowling club and pub A+++.
We paid for an extra night the 6 hours later we wanted to cancel the extra night and they would not even entertain the idea. Never go there again.
Really rude owner kicks kids out for no reason Never coming here again.
An excellent spot for a weekend away. Close to Shoalhaven River and beach. Lots to see and do for kids too.

Took the kids here for a few nights to give mummy a rest from all of us.
Booked an ensuite site for our caravan.
We got 195 and was close to all amenities including the jumping pillow which is the kid's favourite.
Was a bit windy this trip but we'll definitely be returning.
Very friendly staff and service too.
Great campsite, the bathrooms were spotless. So clean! Dog friendly, right by the beach, there are a few shops in town for supplies. Lovely, chilled location.

They have a doggy and a non doggy section. We were in the dog part. A gate next to us gave us access to the beach and the river. Awesome position. Great facilities. Grass sites where we were. A sleepy hollow. A few small local stores. Enough to get you by.
Had a great stay.
One of the nicest caravan parks I've ever visited

I really enjoyed the cleanliness of the cabin and on site facilities.
However, compared to other Holiday Havens - I was disappointed at Shoalhaven Heads esp as an award winning park.

I feel their park planning is poor, the cabins at other HH parks are usually situated away from the ugly permanent vans, but at this one it’s situated right next to permanent vans and camp sites. It’s so unsightly and loud.

The single beds are also very uncomfortable, sagging in the middle with the feeling of wire underneath. My kids didn’t want to sleep in them and so we had to squish on the queen bed. Also no tissue box, unmade single beds, cold thin blankets, and a boxy tiny shower in our 4pp cabin.
It was a lovely place to stay. Clean and tidy rooms, the staff were very accommodating. We enjoyed our stay, thank you.

This is our go to park. The park is located only 40 minutes from home so it's handy if either one of us has to come back for work.
Staff and owners are friendly and always happy to help.
Highly recommend this park.
Camp_trips_with_kids

This place is amazing!!! This was my  second time came to this place .. the cabin that we had was really nice and clean and cosy,  staffs are lovely and helpful when we asked for help and they immediately came to solve the issue for us. thank you Berlinda and other staff for your excellent hospitality service.  Daniel and I will definitely come back again .
was on a power site. lots of space and a extra bonous close to water
We spent 3 nights there. Great place plenty activities to do for the grandchildren. Only metres away from the beach which has a Surf club look out tower and small coffee shop.

This has become our go to park for our long weekends and holidays! Great value and always accomodating. We take our camper trailer and the sites are open and easy to access. Great park for kids and is literally opposite the beach and Shoalhaven river!
Best location, lovely staff. Plenty of activities and well maintain facilities.
Always a great holiday destination we have been traveling here for 10+ years

This location has something for everyone.  The powered sites were plentiful although some are very limited in size and we saw some major issues with the reversing of caravans onto sites.  Easy access to the beach and an awesome pool to cool off in.  Bathroom facilities were well maintained.  Plenty of activities for the kids.

My boyfriend & I had a little weekend getaway here this weekend. We were impressed. Our cabin was neat, tidy & cozy. The air con was an added bonus & so was the bbq on our deck. We also enjoyed the pool table & the swimming pool. Staff was friendly & helpful & so were other guests.
We really needed this mini break & the only terrible part was leaving lol.

Lovely place to stay, we were on site 373 near the beach and river. it has a resort type feel with how beautiful the pool area is. Amenities are spotless. Love that it has a bath for kids. Baths are one thing kids miss A lot when living on the road so it's a real treat to see a caravan park with a bath. Reception staff were amazing and super helpful and friendly. The park is run with high standards and they treat people with kindnes. Sites are generous and well marked out. This is not the first time we have stayed and we will definitely be back. Excellent location sooo soooo soooo much for kids to do. Pump track across the road with a skatepark. 3 playgrounds within walking distance, jumping pillow, beautiful pool with baby pool that has splashing jets. River on one side beach on the other, a short stroll to shops. Dog friendly! The town of shoalhaven heads is quiet and safe, we have family in the area and it's our favourite place to stay when we come to visit.
5 star everything thanks for an amazing stay.

Enjoyed our stay at Shoalhaven Heads , Caravan Park is Pet Friendly, Clean and Quiet, Our site could have done with some TLC and the amenities are showing some wear and tear, Good beach access to 7 mile beach , Overall pretty good park

Update: We returned this year for 2 nights and still just as amazing !!! Can't wait to the full week when we can get time off. Thank you team, we will see you next year!

Only went for the day to see family but loved it! Will be back again next year for a full week!

Wish onpeak I could bring my dogs though but there must be a reason why it's not allowed
Stayed at the cabins. Belinda was so helpful.

Wir waren ein paar Tage hier.
Eigentlich ein schöner Camping, jedoch haben wir folgendes zu bemängeln:
- die Herren Toiletten und Duschen sind sehr alt und riechen oft streng. (bei den Damen wurde alles schön renoviert)
- man hat kaum Handy-Empfang (mit Opus) und das WLAN geht sehr oft nicht.
Have our own Van it is wonderful at the caravan park

Did a load of washing at 12/12.30pm this morning it is now 8.30pm and thanks to a rainy day I still have wet washing. I can not believe there are 2 dryers for the amount of people that are here over the Christmas holidays.
This has to be a joke.

The park has potential to be nice if they were to get rid of all the old ugly permanent vans and invested some money into developing that side of the park with more activities for kids.  If you are on that side of the park you are right amongst all the permanents and pool and kids equipment are ages away on other side of the park, also no dump point on that side either.  I would consider staying elsewhere.
Great position in the van section, views of the water. Amenities were clean, neat, modern. Hot tip for the best food, go to the pub.
Great caravan park. Close to shops and restaurants with a lake on one side and beach on the other

Excellent stay at the the Holiday park.
Great cabin , clean, comfortable and quiet.
Would not hesitate in recommending it for a pleasant get away.

Ken Doust
Beautiful  place as I visited family for the morning.

Highly recommend staying elsewhere if possible, this campsite should be your last resort. The men’s bathroom doesn’t even lock, you need a code to get in but anyone can use their code and access it while someone else is in there because the lock is backwards.
Also, the man mowing went over a pretty decent sized rock and it hit my car. He didn’t stop or anything. Ridiculous campsite to stay at.
Great family venue! Close to pump track, beach  parks and cafes. Park has jumping pillow, park, pool and tennis court. Clean park. Great family facilities
Had a great stay here. Our site was right by the beach. Nice and quiet off peak!

River & Beach access.
Heated pool
Clean amenities
Family friendly
Good clean park.  Kids had a ball.

Lovely flat campsite. Quiet at night. Women’s bathrooms are more modern than mens. Hubby said men’s had the best shower pressure. They put the footy on in the outdoor hut so that was fun. Walked through to the beach and made a circuit past a lagoon. Short drive to Berry and several wineries. Two figs was the fanciest. Mount Ridge - could take it or leave it. Was late to get to Coolongatta winery, but enjoyed a palette tasting on the lawn. Didn’t try the pool but there were plenty of kids in there and the playground (which did have a squeaky swing disturbing a bit if the peace.)😂 Git some messages from managers telling us of activities- eg a food truck came on Saturday night which was awesome.
Stayed 11 days here in my caravan very friendly place and great facilities will definitely be back and highly recommend
Great caravan Park with great facilities to keep the kids entertained. Located between the lake and the beach.
Older type caravan park. Good facilities,  if a little dated. Well maintained games rooms and BBQ facilities.

Great location - but - at $50+ per night for a powered site, the ablution facilities were pretty ordinary - examples include - poorly lit inside, cracked tiles, flaking paint, small/basic showers -essentially old style stuff that was ok in 70's. Just in case you think I am just a whinger, 2 days before we stayed at a smaller park in Port Stephens which had better sites, clean modern individual toilet/shower rooms in the Ablution Block, and was 20% cheaper per night!|Certainly the rest of the facilities seemed to be good, but overall the stay was below par for $50+/night
Great place with water (river) views.
Stade in caravan not room although they looked very nice parkways very good code was required for every thing. Will be staying there again .

This is a well located park, very well kept, and staff that went the extra mile to accommodate our long van. Busy for the long weekend, but no dramas. Close to the beach and river with a bakery and shops close by. It's very windy at times despite tree sheltering. Worth a visit with reasonable fees for van sites
